finding: kiro-cli 2.0 has headless mode with --no-interactive and API key auth. Also has session management that auto-saves. Key blog post at kiro.dev/blog/cli-2-0/ mentions piping inputs and scripting outputs.
KB confirms: --trust-all-tools required for --no-interactive mode. Tool scoping via explicit lists reduces overhead 52-71%. No KB data on persistent/interactive stdin mode.
kiro-cli has --no-interactive, --resume, --resume-id, --agent flags. Current acp.py spawns one process per call with semaphore(10). No --session or --persistent flag exists.
AWS docs confirm context compaction exists for Q CLI - auto-compacts when approaching limits, preserves tool access. Agent config at ~/.aws/amazonq/cli-agents
kiro-cli subagents support up to 4 parallel, have DAG task dependencies, and auto-return results via summary tool. Key for orchestration pattern.

kiro-cli chat --no-interactive is the headless mode. Supports --trust-all-tools, --agent flags. Stdin pipe works with dash: cat prompt.md | kiro-cli chat - BUT it opens/closes immediately without TTY. Custom agents defined in ~/.kiro/agents/ as JSON with locked-down tool lists.
CRITICAL: kiro-cli has ACP (Agent Client Protocol) subcommand - likely supports persistent agent sessions via protocol. Also has --resume/--resume-id for session persistence.

AWS docs confirm Q CLI became Kiro CLI. Redirects to kiro.dev/docs/cli. Agent config at ~/.aws/amazonq/cli-agents. CLI supports --no-interactive, --agent flags. Context compaction at 80% capacity auto-triggers.

Ralph Loop pattern is the key: while :; do cat PROMPT.md | agent -p; done. Fresh context per iteration, filesystem as memory. Directly applicable to kiro-cli with --no-interactive. Each iteration = one debate turn.

kiro-cli agent config is JSON with prompt, tools, mcpServers, hooks (agentSpawn, stop, preToolUse, postToolUse), allowedTools, resources. Stop hooks can intercept agent exit - KEY for never-stop pattern. Compaction auto-triggers on context overflow.

CRITICAL: kiro-cli has ACP mode (Agent Client Protocol) - JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout for persistent connections. botctl uses kiro-cli acp for persistent worker pools. KiroClaw uses single kiro-cli acp process serving all sessions. Context auto-compacts on overflow. Sessions survive process recycling.

Bedrock AgentCore harness supports persistent stateful agents with MCP tools, memory, filesystem. Sessions auto-terminate after 15min idle. Uses Strands Agents framework. Could be alternative to kiro-cli for persistent workers.
CRITICAL: kiro-cli --resume-id WORKS with --no-interactive. Can resume a session, send new message, get response. This enables pseudo-persistent agents by reusing session IDs across calls.

IAM Identity Center supports extended sessions for Kiro up to 90 days. With Builder ID auth, no such limit documented. Kiro CLI docs at kiro.dev/docs/cli/ (external). CloudShell docs confirm kiro-cli command name.

BOTCTL has full worker pool implementation: acp-proxy crate manages kiro-cli pool with max_workers, idle_timeout_secs, session routing, auto-respawn. Config: [agent] command=kiro-cli acp, max_workers=5, idle_timeout_secs=300. Each session maps to a kiro-cli worker. Workers auto-respawn on crash.

BREAKTHROUGH: kiro-cli interactive mode accepts MULTIPLE messages via stdin pipe in SAME session. Tested: piped 2 questions, got 2 answers with context preserved. Response delimiter is ESC]9;Response complete BEL. This enables persistent agent pattern!

AMek is a purpose-built async task queue for AI agents with MCP integration. Pull-based claim model (agents claim tasks, not assigned). Split principal identity (alias,agentName). States: OPEN→CLAIMED→DELIVERED→CLOSED. Has kiro-cli MCP tools: post_task, list_tasks, claim, deliver, close. Perfect queue pattern for debate system.

BREAKTHROUGH: kiro-cli interactive mode STAYS ALIVE when reading from a FIFO with write-end kept open. Sent 2+2, got 4. Then sent 7+7, got 14. SAME PROCESS, SAME SESSION. This enables true persistent agents via named pipes!

CRITICAL: kiro-cli chat flags confirmed: --no-interactive (runs without user input), --agent <name>, --trust-all-tools, --resume/--resume-id (session persistence!), --wrap never, --require-mcp-startup. Agent format is JSON with name/description/mcpServers/prompt/tools fields at ~/.kiro/agents/. INPUT arg is the first question.

BREAKTHROUGH: kiro-cli has --resume-id flag! Can send follow-up messages to same session. Pattern: start session, get ID, then resume-id for each work item. Session maintains context + MCP tools across calls. This is the persistent worker pattern WITHOUT needing a truly persistent process.

COMPLETE Python ACP client pattern found in 10+ repos. Key: spawn kiro-cli acp as subprocess with stdin/stdout pipes, JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol. Methods: initialize→session/new→session/prompt (loop). Auto-approve permissions. Context auto-compacts at 90%. Session survives process recycling via session/load. Multiple sessions per process NOT supported - need 1 process per worker.
CONFIRMED: Persistent kiro-cli via FIFO retains FULL TOOL ACCESS. Agent created a file using fs_write tool in persistent FIFO mode. Tools work across multiple messages in same session.

FINAL: Ralph Loop is the answer. Use bash while loop + kiro-cli --no-interactive per iteration. Fresh context each turn (no degradation). Filesystem queue with atomic mv for locking. 4-10 workers managed by Python orchestrator. Community-validated pattern (work-loop skill, 163 stars). Estimated 10x speedup over sequential.

ACP is THE answer: kiro-cli acp communicates via stdin/stdout JSON-RPC. Methods: session/new, session/prompt, session/load. Supports persistent sessions, context compaction notifications, tool calls. This is the programmatic interface for persistent agents!

KEY SETTINGS: chat.disableAutoCompaction=true (currently disabled!), mcp.noInteractiveTimeout=30000 (30s timeout for non-interactive), mcp.initTimeout=200ms. The noInteractiveTimeout is the max time a --no-interactive call can run before timing out.

Context compaction is AUTOMATIC when window overflows. Summarizes older messages, retains recent ones. Creates new session but can resume original. This means persistent agents can run indefinitely - compaction handles context overflow.

CONFIRMED: stdin pipe works with --no-interactive. echo 'prompt' | kiro-cli chat --no-interactive --trust-all-tools exits normally and returns response. Output ends with ANSI sequence + response text + Credits line.

CONFIRMED NEGATIVE: --resume-id does NOT restore conversation history into LLM context in --no-interactive mode. Agent said 'I don't have any information' when asked about secret number from previous session. Memory must be passed explicitly in prompt.
CONFIRMED: kiro-cli agent format is JSON (not YAML). Located at ~/.kiro/agents/*.json. Fields: name, description, prompt, tools, allowedTools, mcpServers, includeMcpJson.

CONFIRMED: Self-polling agent pattern viable. Agent with shell tools can run 'while true; do ls queue/pending/*.json && process; sleep 5; done' as a shell command. kiro-cli stays alive while shell command runs.
CONFIRMED: kiro-cli --list-sessions shows sessions with UUID, timestamp, first message preview, message count, and store version (v1/v2). Sessions are directory-scoped.

INVESTIGATION COMPLETE. Full findings written. Key answer: Use kiro-cli acp (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout) for persistent workers. Python orchestrator sends prompts to persistent kiro-cli processes. No need for LLM to poll - orchestrator dispatches work. 10+ production repos use this pattern. Botctl has full worker pool with max_workers, idle_timeout, auto-respawn. Context auto-compacts at 90%.
FINAL: Complete findings written. ACP protocol is the answer - JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout, persistent sessions, auto-compaction, all MCP tools available. Implementation plan with code included.
